title: financial stability / nonbank finance / regulatory gaps | Research Paper | Task Force on Financial Stability
summary: The Brookings Task Force on Financial Stability, co-chaired by Glenn Hubbard and Donald Kohn, released a June 2021 report examining systemic risks in the U.S. financial system. The report focuses on vulnerabilities exposed during March 2020 market disruptions, identifying significant fragility in the nonbank finance sector despite resilient banking reforms from the post-2008 era. The task force proposes regulatory recommendations to address gaps in oversight of nonbank finance, open-end funds, clearinghouses, and Treasury market liquidity.
